title: Debugging Tests
description: Master debugging techniques and tools in Playwright

questions.question: Which tool provides step-by-step debugging in Playwright?
questions.explanation: Playwright supports debugging through Inspector, Chrome DevTools, and VS Code debugger.

questions.question: Complete the code to pause test execution for debugging:
questions.explanation: page.pause() pauses test execution and opens Playwright Inspector for debugging.

questions.question: Playwright can capture screenshots and videos of test execution.
questions.explanation: Playwright can automatically capture screenshots and videos, especially useful for debugging failures.

questions.question: What does the --debug flag do?
questions.explanation: The --debug flag opens Playwright Inspector, allowing step-by-step test execution and inspection.

questions.question: Complete the code to take a screenshot:
questions.explanation: page.screenshot() captures a screenshot of the current page state.

questions.question: The Trace Viewer allows you to replay test execution step by step.
questions.explanation: Trace Viewer is a powerful tool that records and replays test execution with full details of each action.

questions.question: How do you enable trace recording on test failure?
questions.explanation: Use 'on-failure' or 'retain-on-failure' in playwright.config to record traces only for failed tests.

questions.question: What's the best way to debug this failing test?
options: Add page.pause() before the failing line
questions.explanation: All methods are valid: pause() for interactive debugging, --debug for step-by-step, traces for post-mortem analysis.

questions.question: Which configuration captures video of test execution?
questions.explanation: Video can be set to 'on' (always record), 'retain-on-failure' (only keep on failure), or 'off' (disabled).

questions.question: You can use console.log() to debug Playwright tests.
questions.explanation: While Playwright has better debugging tools, console.log() can still be used for simple debugging needs.
